Any country that imposes a digital services tax on US firms, he wrote, would face “a 100% TARIFF on […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Peec AI doubles its valuation betting GEO is the new SEO https://thenextweb.com/news/peec-ai-200m-valuation-geo-ai-search Mon, 29 Jun 2026 09:50:24 +0000 TheNextWeb=46ba419546be2b51c793995ab343b18a For 20 years, marketing meant one thing: rank on Google. That deal is breaking. A Berlin startup has just doubled its valuation on a bet about what comes next. Peec AI is in talks to raise new funding at a pre-money valuation of $200M, Sifted reported, citing three sources. That is roughly double its worth […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> ETH Zurich’s bidirectional pixel could turn screens into cameras https://thenextweb.com/news/eth-zurichs-bidirectional-pixel-could-turn-screens-into-cameras Mon, 29 Jun 2026 09:41:55 +0000 TheNextWeb=7ecdac483fe54d501d63949805f1d24f A pixel has always done one job. On a screen it emits light to build a picture. In a camera it absorbs light to record one. A team in Switzerland has now made one that does both. Researchers at ETH Zurich have built the first bidirectional pixel, in work published in Nature. The same tiny […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Tesla settles a fatal Full Self-Driving crash lawsuit https://thenextweb.com/news/tesla-fsd-fatal-crash-settlement-nhtsa Mon, 29 Jun 2026 09:33:08 +0000 TheNextWeb=0139f78123c3d6aed45a5e33411c763e A settlement is the sound a lawsuit makes when it stops. For Tesla, one just went quiet. The far louder problem, a federal safety investigation, is still talking. Tesla has settled a lawsuit tied to a fatal 2023 crash involving its Full Self-Driving (Supervised) system, Bloomberg first reported. Neither side disclosed the terms. TechCrunch laid […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> SpaceX wants its own gas pipeline to fuel Starship https://thenextweb.com/news/spacex-starpipe-natural-gas-starship Mon, 29 Jun 2026 09:20:35 +0000 TheNextWeb=87223b36dc521b28f73a6fa6cb530540 Most rocket companies buy their fuel. SpaceX wants to pipe it in. Filings in Texas show the company plans to build its own natural gas pipeline, an unusual move for a space firm, and a telling one. SpaceX calls the line Starpipe. It would run eight miles, about 13km, to Starbase, SpaceX’s company town on […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> AWS raises GPU prices 20% as the memory crunch bites https://thenextweb.com/news/aws-gpu-prices-increase-memory-shortage Mon, 29 Jun 2026 09:07:17 +0000 TheNextWeb=8be00655e698d66639db574074c169a2 Renting an AI chip is starting to feel like booking a hotel in a sold-out city. You pay to hold the room, and the rate keeps climbing. On AWS, it just climbed again. Amazon Web Services has raised prices for EC2 Capacity Blocks for ML by roughly 20%, starting in July. The pitch: sell the self-improving AI that the big labs build for themselves and guard from everyone else. The biggest AI labs share one private conviction. The fastest way to build better AI is to point […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> OpenAI hires Apple’s Vision Pro chief Paul Meade https://thenextweb.com/news/openai-hires-apple-vision-pro-chief Mon, 29 Jun 2026 08:39:07 +0000 TheNextWeb=6129ea41187821cde7ac336f7dbc1849 Paul Meade, Apple’s Vision Pro chief, is leaving to build OpenAI’s devices. It is the most senior Apple defection yet, and it points the AI hardware talent war straight at Cupertino. Apple does not lose vice presidents to rivals. That is the unwritten rule, and for years it held. Designers slipped away to Jony Ive’s […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Germany’s AI rollout is being sold as a fix for its worker shortage https://thenextweb.com/news/germanys-ai-rollout-is-being-sold-as-a-fix-for-its-worker-shortage Mon, 29 Jun 2026 08:28:15 +0000 TheNextWeb=21dbfd55cc09194fb629a92641d9d5c4 The case for artificial intelligence in Germany is being made, increasingly, in the language of arithmetic rather than ambition. The country does not have enough workers, and AI is being pitched as a way to need fewer of them. The concrete version of that pitch is small and unglamorous. A homebuilder in the northwest of […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Nearly 400 local newspapers sue OpenAI and Microsoft over copyright https://thenextweb.com/news/local-newspapers-sue-openai-microsoft-copyright Mon, 29 Jun 2026 08:26:22 +0000 TheNextWeb=35cda006bb0fc2b3350813b133e118fb A coalition that owns nearly 400 local US newspapers has sued OpenAI and Microsoft. The publishers call AI training on their reporting a death knell for local journalism. It is the largest copyright case the local press has brought yet. Local newspapers report the meetings no algorithm attends. The council vote, the school board row, […] This story continues at The Next Web]]>
